description_long: This is a small, single-propeller military training airplane with a sleek, streamlined fuselage painted primarily in white with bold red accents on the nose, tail, and wing tips. The aircraft features a low-wing configuration with straight wings and a single vertical stabilizer at the tail, both adorned with military insignia and identification markings. The cockpit is enclosed with a clear canopy, positioned forward on the fuselage, and appears to be designed for one or two occupants. The landing gear is of the tricycle type, with two main wheels under the wings and a single nose wheel, all of which are fixed and visible. The propeller is mounted at the nose, indicating a single-engine, tractor configuration. The overall design suggests a robust, maneuverable aircraft suitable for training or light utility roles in an urban or airfield environment.
description: A small, single-propeller military training airplane with a white and red color scheme, low straight wings, and a forward enclosed cockpit. It features fixed tricycle landing gear and prominent military markings.
description_view_0: Side view showing the propeller, forward cockpit canopy, fixed tricycle landing gear, and military insignia on the fuselage and wing.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the nose-mounted propeller, straight low wings, and symmetrical landing gear arrangement.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the tail number, vertical stabilizer, and the red-tipped wings and tail surfaces.
description_view_3: Rear view emphasizing the tail section, single vertical stabilizer, and the even span of the wings.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, straight configuration with no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted propeller engine (likely piston or turboprop).
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed tricycle landing gear with two main wheels and one nose wheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a clear canopy, likely single or tandem seating.
